title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers in Brookline voted to authorize a strike as union contract talks remain stalled.
Negotiations have focused on job protection and self-service kiosks, with workers seeking greater protections.
The theater's management emphasizes ongoing good-faith negotiations, with no immediate strike planned.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora predicts Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 season.
FanGraphs projects no Red Sox player to hit over 20 home runs, with Abreu expected to hit 18.
Abreu hit 22 home runs and drove in 69 runs in 115 games during the 2025 season.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots predicted to select Arizona State's Max Iheanachor with the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Max Iheanachor, a 6-foot-6, 321-pound offensive tackle, started at Arizona State for two seasons and earned Second Team All-Big 12 honors.
Patriots seek long-term offensive tackle replacement for veteran Morgan Moses who recently turned 35, despite his continued high-level play.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester officials condemned racist comments made online after a girl's basketball game between South High and Andover High.
The NAACP and YWCA criticized threats against South High players, urging community action against racism and cyberbullying.
A video clip of the game received over 2 million views, prompting backlash for its portrayal of South High athletes.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, of Rehoboth, was charged with four counts of sexual exploitation of minors, according to federal prosecutors.
Authorities found and seized a phone with child sexual abuse material during Mota's border search at Logan International Airport.
Mota faces 15 to 30 years in prison if convicted, alongside potential fines and supervised release, prosecutors said.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened after water main break caused flooding and road closures.
A 12-inch diameter pipe rupture led to flooding; full shutdown and repairs completed within 48 hours.
Nearby businesses, including three daycares, were evacuated and closed until repairs were finished.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Derrick Todd, 52, indicted on three rape and 17 indecent assault charges related to 11 patients in Framingham.
Todd allegedly assaulted women aged 20-60 from 2021-2023 while at Charles River Medical Associates.
Todd's previous charges include two rape counts; over 200 patients joined a class action lawsuit against him.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: AAA Northeast's Car Doctor suggests adding a second battery for van conversion, recommending lithium iron phosphate for better performance.
For electronics and appliances, use a pure sine wave converter and determine wattage needs, typically 1,000–2,000 watts.
Charge an auxiliary battery with a vehicle alternator or rooftop solar panel, with a voltage regulator ensuring consistent power supply.

title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Charges against former Brockton choral director Matthew Cunningham were dropped as the alleged victim declined to testify.
Cunningham was arrested in August 2025 for allegedly paying an underage boy for sex, initially charged with trafficking and sexual conduct for a fee.
Cunningham is no longer employed by Brockton Public Schools, having been on leave since his arrest in 2025.

title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old were arrested for armed robbery in Charlestown, involving an elderly victim.
The suspects allegedly pointed a gun at the victim, stole her purse, and fled the scene near Monument Square.
Jordan pleaded not guilty and is held without bail; the 14-year-old's name is withheld due to age.
